As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the latest series of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Jo Brand, Olivia Colman, Stacey Dooley, Aston Merrygold, Diane Morgan, Bob Mortimer, Richard Osman, Lucy Porter, Jon Richardson, Henning Wehn and Shaun Williamson to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Paul Sinha, Lou Sanders and Iain Stirling become one while Greg Davies watches on, ready to issue his judgements, while Sian Gibson seeks to redefine the definition of 'bounce'

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Extended edition of the satirical current affairs quiz. Rev, Doctor Thorne and The Night Manager star Tom Hollander hosts, with comedian Hal Cruttenden and UKIP leadership candidate Suzanne Evans joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's news

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts a special edition that poses questions on all manner of inventions, including some that proved to be 'just the job'. Trying to come up with interesting answers are Top Gear presenter Jeremy Clarkson, comedian Jason Manford, broadcaster Sandi Toksvig and regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig 'quarrels', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Jason Manford, Aisling Bea and Anuvab Pal at the brunt of her angst. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct

programme.title: Porters
programme.desc: The annual Hospital Emergency Simulation Incident Day arrives, but after being accidentally knocked out Simon wakes up in a real disaster. Frankie goes for a secret job interview

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: A fed-up claimant appears in court to stop his friend climbing on their roof and a teacher accuses a colleague of theft in a dramatic legal showdown

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In the Jebel Akhdar mountain range of Oman, Steve leads an expedition into an unexplored desert canyon. With temperatures soaring, dehydration and heat stroke are a constant threat

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Teams battle against the clock in an extreme downhill race at the beautiful landscaped gardens of Villa Borghese, Rome

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and David Baddiel travel along more than 1,200 miles of hazardous roads in Ethiopia on a journey to Aksum, the country's holiest place. However, they have to contend with more than fatigue and unreliable terrain to reach their destination, as they encounter drug-taking truck drivers, suffer a disastrous breakdown and start to run out of fuel at the worst possible time

programme.title: Top Gear: From A-Z
programme.desc: John Bishop narrates an alphabetical romp through the series' most memorable stunts and features as former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May pushed vehicles of all shapes and sizes to their limits. With 22 seasons of the show, there was no shortage of material to choose from as the likes of Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz attempted to be the fastest Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and the presenters risked life and limb in their assorted globe-trotting adventures

programme.title: Top Gear: Winter Blunderland
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May present their best winter challenges, including playing ice hockey with Suzukis and what occurs if you ski-jump a rocket-powered Mini

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Special in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three used, V8-engined sports cars on a gruelling road trip covering 1,600 miles through Patagonia to the world's southernmost city. Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: The 2018 edition of the series in which teams of amateur racers test out their homemade, gravity-powered soapboxes on obstacle-littered courses in Johannesburg, South Africa. The entrants create vehicles without engines and race downhill in this popular offbeat event

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Lucy Porter, Frankie Boyle and Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'next' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig 'quarrels', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Jason Manford, Aisling Bea and Anuvab Pal at the brunt of her angst. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents a special edition looking back over the best moments from the series. programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. Guests Miranda Hart, Stephen Mangan, Barry Cryer and Miles Jupp join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Lou Sanders chews on stationery, Sian Gibson is crushed by a caravan, Paul Sinha wrecks a camera, Joe Thomas gets cross and Iain Stirling waves a stick about

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts an extended edition of the satirical current affairs quiz from December 2016, with Citizen Khan star Adil Ray and Conservative MP Jacob Rees-Mogg joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's news

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Question master Stephen Fry continues the comedy panel quiz's exploration of subjects beginning with the letter L as he asks a range of fiendish questions on the topic of ladies and gents. programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos and Claudia Winkleman. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: Paul Danan finally gets his day in court with Shaun Williamson after a pantomime-based fallout, while a husband is sick and tired of his miserly wife ruining Christmas

programme.title: Judge Romesh
programme.desc: A desperate father begs Judge Romesh not to let his son run off to the circus and a mother is sick and tired of her daughter bringing 'bad juju' into the house

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How in devising the F35 Lightnin II, engineers have pushed the boundaries of aviation technology further than ever before and created a brand new, revolutionary, fighter aircraft capable of handling threats in the air and on the ground. Plus, Rob Collings flies one of the most successful aircraft of the Second World War, the distinctive P38, former RAF test pilot Andy Edgell tracks down the product of a top secret research project, the X-14 and retired US Air Force pilot Mace Carpenter reconnects with the stealthy F117

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germany's plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: British bombers are unable to hit their targets precisely and attack German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo undertakes the last leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. He travels to Perran Sands, where he searches for the lost church of St Piran, a place believed to be one of the earliest Christian sites in England. In Redruth, he explores the remains of the Cornish tin industry, before heading to St Ives, where he spends the night in Treganna Castle. The former politician finishes his trip in Penzance, discovering how railways link remote communities to the rest of the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey to London from Buxton, Derbyshire, where he discovers how railways inspired a building craze and boosted tourism. In the Peak District, he joins a team of volunteers repairing damage done to ancient peat landscapes during the Industrial Revolution, and near the quarries of Stanton Moor he learns how trains transported Derbyshire's famous sandstone all over England

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that 'goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications'

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn follows his family's roots by exploring Metroland, the area around the Metropolitan Railway, and in Great Malvern learns about the woman behind a decorative 1860s station

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A seemingly endless concrete line stretching through the French countryside, which sits as a monument to a discarded engineering advancement

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A labyrinth of tunnels underneath a tiny French village which contains mysteries from several historical eras, and a Welsh slate mine that has been given a new lease of life

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the incredible machines built to get troops directly to the front line, on land, at sea and in the air

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: David Bowie: Finding Fame
programme.desc: The final part of Francis Whately's trilogy of films on Bowie's life, telling the story of the five years when many of the ideas that would help him become an icon were first born. Starting in 1966, soon after David Jones changed his name to David Bowie, the film traces his interest in everything from Holst and Pinky and Perky to Anthony Newley and Tibetan Buddhism, and how he used all of these influences to create not only Ziggy Stardust, but the material for his entire career

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Recalling a decade in British music, with archive performances from the early stages of the 'swingin' era, featuring music by by rock, pop and R'n'B artists including the Springfields, the Shadows, the Beatles and the Rolling Stones

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: More pop, rock and rhythm and blues from the archives, featuring appearances by the Rolling Stones, the Dave Clark Five, the Seekers and the Byrds from 1964 and '65

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A selection of archive footage from the arrival of the so-called beat boom in 1964, which catered to the art-school craze for rhythm and blues. Featuring performances by Tom Jones, the Kinks, Manfred Mann, the Rolling Stones, John Lee Hooker and the Pretty Things

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage
programme.desc: The Goblin Works Garage gang opens a bigger workshop to cope with demand, going bolder than ever before with design and restoration work

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage
programme.desc: The team takes on its first ever EV build as its members attempt to mount a Tesla battery-powered electric powerplant inside a classic - the Cinquecento or Fiat 500

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage
programme.desc: The gang transforms an iconic 1973 Ford Bronco into a monster truck with attitude. Plus, Ant builds a bike for his most important client: his daughter

programme.title: Goblin Works Garage
programme.desc: The team take on a Japanese sports car legend - the Toyota Supra. These cars have been modified countless times, so how can they make theirs stand out?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer wants to splash the cash on a custom bus, but mechanic Edd China favours a more conservative campervan due to him being an air-cooling expert. the two settle for a VW Split-Screen

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer purchases a Mini Moke, which was designed as an off-road military vehicle, for expert mechanic Edd China to work on - before trying to sell it for a profit

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Edd China work on an old Aston Martin DB7, hoping to restore it to its former glory but hidden problems threaten to blow their budget

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: After purchasing a badly modified DeSoto Firedome, Mike Brewer and Edd China set about addressing the damage to give the stunning cruiser back some of its class

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer finds a Humvee to work on, and he and Edd China create a four-wheel-drive camping vehicle capable of venturing to places no camper has been before

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a silver art nouveau mirror, a leather writing case once owned by Robert Stevenson, and an ancient recording machine designed by Thomas Edison

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Upholsterer Craig is tested to his limit by an Edwardian sofa ruined by an amateur repair job. Plus, lighting restorer Jolene tackles an original Second World War bomber lamp

programme.desc: Cabinet maker Alex repairs a battered chest that once belonged to a secretive Masonic lodge. Blacksmith Nev uses his skills to restore some historic street lamps

programme.desc: Fine art sculptor Nick must be extra careful as he uses a drill to restore a 19th-century urn. Lighting specialist Jolene is challenged by Drew to repair a funfair display

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Sculptor Nick has his skills stretched by an equestrian piece with classical connections. Plus, leather expert Martin works on a piece of luggage once owned by Russian royalty

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Blacksmith Neville has his expert skills tested with the restoration of a cast iron 19th-century fireplace. Plus, Alex restores a 1930s table into a farmhouse centrepiece

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Salvage supremo Drew Pritchard is spoiled for choice at a dealership in Henley, Oxfordshire. He meets a good match in Marlborough, Wiltshire, and tries to score a win against the house at a bingo hall

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory in time for Christmas. It moves sisters Ann and Kathryn to tears when they hear it play the family's favourite carol for the first time in decades

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: Part one of two. Paul Jr makes a decision that could end his estrangement from Paul Sr. Meanwhile, PJD starts a build for Skil Tools and OCC works on a bike based on a sports car

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Costa Diadema is a Dream-class cruise ship owned by Carnival Corporation and operated by Costa Crociere. The ship was ordered in October and delivered to Costa exactly four years later. It gives its 3,700 passengers the ultimate Mediterranean experience as Captain Giorgio Moretti uses his 37-year experience to guide this 306-metre-long vessel

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: Exploring the inner workings of the Skandi Arctic, the world's largest and most technologically advanced dive support ship

programme.desc: A look at how customised knee replacements, leaf springs and lavender oil are made

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers need to impress judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles with their pastry-plaiting skills, in a challenge that offers the contenders of Heat A their last chance to go through to Finals week. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire fell in love with Bulgaria 30 years ago when she was working as a teacher, and now she can't wait to buy a holiday home there. She loves the traditional town of Tryavna's authentic atmosphere and beautiful countryside, and is after a three-bedroom house for her budget of £40,000. Jonnie Irwin shows Sarah and her best friend Suzie five properties to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Gary and Susie, who want to start a new adventure in Florida's beautiful Sarasota Keys. For their budget of £220,000 they're after a place to escape the British winters, as close to the amazing beaches of the Gulf of Mexico as possible. Jonnie shows them how far their budget will stretch in this highly desirable corner of the US

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: White witch Maureen McKissock, whose house contains books of spells, shelves full of crystals and an earless cat, kicks off the competition in Ayr by preparing a curry with a potent cocktail of spices. However, one of the guests voices her objections to the main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: College lecturer Elaine Munro hosts the second party in Ayr, hoping her culinary creation of Scottish salmon wrapped in Parma ham is an improvement on Maureen McKissock's spicy offerings. She also tries to impress by offering the guests champagne on their arrival - but businessman Forbes Robertson decides he would prefer a cup of tea

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Casino owner Forbes Robertson takes a gamble by serving up pigs' trotters which cost just 30p each. He splashes out on champagne, hoping to put his guests in high spirits, but it remains to be seen whether his down-to-earth choice will satisfy his diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lizzy McCubbin hosts an upmarket dinner party in Ayr and plans to impress her guests with a lavish four-course meal and first-class presentation. In a bid to stop smokers Elaine Munro and Sarah Pickering from leaving the table for cigarette breaks, she presents them with a gift of nicotine gum, but the gesture is not well received

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nature-lover Sarah Pickering hosts the final dinner party of the week in Ayr, with a menu featuring soup, home-made ice-cream and a flying Scotsman - chicken stuffed with haggis and wrapped in Ayrshire bacon. The meal takes a lot of preparation, allowing the guests ample time to consider what score they will give

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is sent to France on a student exchange, where he is used for slave labour by corrupt wine-makers. Back in Springfield, his Albanian replacement takes an unhealthy interest in the nuclear power plant

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart inadvertently exposes Krusty the Clown as one of the worst tax-dodgers in history and faces the trauma of seeing his TV idol prosecuted for fraud

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet a woman whose family have raced cars for three generations, and Simon comes across an old ship's carpenter's chest he can restore

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: The Winners
programme.desc: The second of two specials paying tribute to the winners from past shows, made to celebrate the series' 10th anniversary. The second programme focuses on the five most recent winners, including Nadiya, Candice and Rahul, reliving their most memorable moments, from their first Signature challenge to their final Showstopper

programme.title: One Night In Hamleys
programme.desc: Three comedians get to live out their childhood fantasies by spending the night in London's famous toy shop, with no security guards to keep them out of mischief. The comics face a series of ridiculous festive games and challenges with only two rules - nobody is allowed to go to sleep and anything they break, they have to pay for

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the words and numbers game, with Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican face off against Sean Lock and David Mitchell, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick, Angel, Dorothy and Arthur, as well as Angel's parents Jenni and Steve prepare to celebrate a family Christmas in France. With an old 19th-century chateau ledger found in the attic and classic cookbooks to hand, the Strawbridges take inspiration from Christmases past, discovering what food, festivities, gifts and games would have been like in bygone eras. Dick and Angel's imagination goes into overdrive as they create a winter wonderland with an ice rink at its heart. Later, as the family settles down to dinner, so the sound of traditional hand bells rings out across the chateau and they wonder if their exploration of the past will create chateau traditions for the future

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere and Warwick Castles
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix pits a leading chocolatier against a virtuoso pastry chef to replicate one of the nation's best-loved festive chocolate brands - Quality Street. As ever, the chefs must work out the secret techniques and recipes behind the household favourites before creating their own versions to be judged by a panel of Quality Street insiders and experts. But with so many to choose from, will they be expected to remake the green triangle, the orange crunch or the iconic purple one?

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Sarah and Graham recruit a band of workers to begin Covid-safe renovation work after a month's pause in the project. The couple disagree on whether they should have animals, so arrange to borrow four alpacas from a nearby farm to see if they like the idea. The farmhouse finally has power, but it still needs running water, so plans get underway to dig a borehole

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: The Winners
programme.desc: The second of two specials paying tribute to the winners from past shows, made to celebrate the series' 10th anniversary. The second programme focuses on the five most recent winners, including Nadiya, Candice and Rahul, reliving their most memorable moments, from their first Signature challenge to their final Showstopper

programme.title: The Tiger Who Came to Tea
programme.desc: Hand-drawn animated version of Judith Kerr's much-loved tale, which celebrated its 50th anniversary in 2018. A tea-guzzling tiger turns up unannounced and eats and drinks young Sophie and her mummy out of everything in the house, even draining the water from their taps. Narrated by David Walliams, with David Oyelowo providing the voice for the big cat alongside seven-year-old Clara Ross as Sophie, with other characters played by Benedict Cumberbatch, Tamsin Greig and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as the next 10 contestants enter the tent to face a technical challenge involving multi-coloured batter, and then have to bake cakes to represent their families

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The Heat B contestants face two biscuit-themed challenges - a sophisticated update of a party favourite and a representation of the best day of their lives

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining young cooks face two tricky challenges on Bread Day, including creating a sculpture centrepiece based on their favourite hobby

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The contestants face a couple of dessert challenges, only for Harry Hill to hijack the technical and ask them to create a unique bake that is both food and art

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Pastry Day for the Heat B bakers and their last chance to win a place in Finals week. Judge Liam's Charles' technical challenge is an unusual hybrid requiring the bakers to demonstrate knowledge and techniques worthy of an advanced pastry chef. Then the bakers are tasked with creating a showstopper in the form of savoury hand pies. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bristol couple Carol and Stephen Howell find a holiday home in Barbados, showing them a number of properties within their £275,000 budget

programme.desc: By Quentin Blake. Animation bringing to life the adventures of a little toy clown, thrown away with a load of old discarded toys, as he goes on a journey to find a new loving home for himself and his friends. Narrated by Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: It's a Wonderful Life (1946)
programme.desc: A hard-working, small-town businessman comes to think his life has been a complete waste of time and tries to commit suicide. At the last minute, his guardian angel pays him a visit and makes him realise his true worth by showing him what would have happened to those around him had he never been born. Fantasy drama, starring James Stewart, Donna Reed, Lionel Barrymore, Henry Travers, Beulah Bondi, Thomas Mitchell and Frank Faylen

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host this festive edition of the culinary competition, with celebrity contestants trying to impress judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix and his team head for the British countryside to celebrate love in the most festive of locations, where 28-year-old Holly goes on a date with 32-year-old Shane. Elsewhere, Paul, who has never had a boyfriend, is set up with 35-year-old IT worker Alex from London, and Shauna and James, who are both from Dewsbury, enjoy a festive date

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at previous first dates that did not go smoothly, including make-up artist Demi's awkward meeting with 29-year-old Danny, and retired NHS counsellor Elaine's date with music producer Steve, which appeared to be going well until it came to settling the bill. Plus, a look back at Disney superfans James and Jess's date

programme.title: A Baby Reindeer's First Christmas
programme.desc: Chances are any reindeer at a festive event has not come from Lapland but from the Cairngorm mountains in Scotland. This documentary follows a dedicated community of herders as they nurture and train their antlered wards - skills include learning to pull the Christmas sleigh - and prepare them for Christmas in a year like no other

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the festive season in Devon and Cornwall with people whose livelihood depends on this busy time of year. The first stop is Cornwall's Lizard peninsula, to meet the Richards family, who have been farming this region for more than 500 years. Cameras also follow Devon artist Luna North, whose love of the region inspires her to paint her own Christmas card

programme.title: Inside John Lewis: Trouble At The Tills
A Royal Winter (2017)
programme.desc: A young woman on a European holiday finds herself in the middle of a real-life fairy tale when a chance meeting with a handsome local leads to something more. Romantic drama, starring Merritt Patterson, Jack Donnelly and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Christmas Encore (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling actress falls in love with her director during a production of A Christmas Carol. Romantic drama, starring Maggie Lawson and Brennan Elliott

programme.title: Christmas at the Palace (2018)
programme.desc: A former professional ice skater is hired by a king to help his daughter in a Christmas skating performance. Romantic drama, starring Merritt Patterson and Andrew Cooper

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart accidentally destroys the family's Christmas presents and evades blame by claiming they were stolen by a burglar, prompting the Springfield residents to open their hearts and wallets in an overwhelming show of charity toward the family. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a doting father and decides to spend quality time with his youngest daughter when he discovers Santa's Little Helper knows Maggie better than he does. Meanwhile, a lonely Moe joins an online dating agency, and gets a pleasant surprise when he meets an attractive woman

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned Flanders buys the Simpsons' home when they are unable to pay their mortgage and rents it back to them at an affordable rate, but Homer soon takes advantage of his new landlord's generosity

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a Gloucestershire couple find a holiday home in Fuerteventura that is large enough to accommodate three generations of their family

Later, as the family settles down to dinner, so the sound of traditional hand bells rings out across the chateau and they wonder if their exploration of the past will create chateau traditions for the future

Christmas dinner with all the trimmings is on the menu in December, meaning the chefs cook an amazing 3,000kg of turkey as well as thousands of sausages and sprouts. However, after receiving news that Olinsky has been stabbed, Voight seeks justice - especially when the prison CCTV footage of an earlier altercation shows a security guard turning a blind eye to proceedings. The guard is squeezed for a lead into Olinsky's killer, who is quickly identified and has his paymaster's name beaten out of him. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: UK Border Force

programme.title: UK Border Force
programme.desc: Officers at Heathrow's Terminal 3 question a passenger from Johannesburg arriving in London on a tourist visa, suspecting he may be planning to work in the country. Entry Clearance Officer Dan Brown, on secondment to the British High Commission in Delhi, encounters a man carrying false identity documents

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A passenger who is carrying clumps of human hair becomes hysterical when her luggage returns positive readings for drugs. Meanwhile, Immigration has concerns about an Indonesian man who is traveling with several disturbing documents

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A drug-runner conceals his stash in the most unlikely of places. Meanwhile, a heartbroken Frenchman has fallen for Australia, but for Immigration it's time to separate, and experienced officers are shocked to discover a foetus in the mail

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Officers make a disturbing discovery in a Chinese student's bag, and a devious passenger will try anything to get past immigration

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: When a woman pushes away a sniffer dog officers hone in on what she has stuffed in her underwear. A young man claims he has arrived on a spiritual journey, but when his dubious past is exposed he must confess. Two Indian passengers will say just about anything to get out of a fine. Fly-on-the-wall documentary following security staff at Australia's biggest airports

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: All the signs point to a passenger being involved in the importation of narcotics, and customs and immigration officers are determined to catch him out. Fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A bungling drug mule tries to avoid being caught, an elderly gambler's luck runs out when Immigration uncover his ongoing scam, and Biosecurity intercept a delivery from Russia

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A woman returns from Vietnam with some suspicious tea, officers are wary of an old-time drug smuggler who wants to enter Australia with his wife, and a middle eastern man is reluctant to give up his smoking tobacco

programme.title: The Queen and I
programme.desc: New republican prime minister Jack Barker strips the royals of their assets and titles and the Queen and her family are forced to move to a rundown council estate. The locals don't exactly roll out the red carpet, but new neighbours Bev, Tony, Violet and Spiggy prove to be unlikely allies. Comedy based on Sue Townsend's book, starring David Walliams, Samantha Bond, Frances Barber, Amanda Abbington, Johnny Vegas and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Cinderella: After Ever After
programme.desc: Cinderella has just married her Prince Charming, but soon has a rude awakening after realising married life isn't all it's cracked up to be when you have an image-obsessed, floss-dancing, hip-hop rapping husband. She also fights to save the King from the scheming of her evil stepmother Madame Blackheart, and her conspiring and moronic stepsisters Dumbella and Rubella. Family comedy, starring Sian Gibson, David Walliams, Tom Courtenay and Celia Imrie

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.desc: Part one of two. The naturalist explores the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years, beginning with a look at how remarkable advances allowed primitive fish to make the radical move on to land, and then take to the skies with the advent of flight. He uses fossils of both ancient and living species combined with CGI and film to chart their journey out of the water to populate all corners of the globe

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: Attenborough's Journey
programme.desc: Documentary following David Attenborough as he journeys around the world to make his series First Life, which explores the origins of life on Earth. As he visits locations including the Great Barrier Reef, the deserts of Morocco and the glaciers of Canada, the broadcaster and wildlife expert explains his life-long fascination with the natural world, and also travels back to Leicestershire, the county in which he spent his childhood

programme.title: Greg Davies: Firing Cheeseballs at a Dog
programme.desc: The Edinburgh Comedy Award-nominated debut solo show by comedian and Inbetweeners star Greg Davies, in which he sets out to chronicle the significant issues surrounding modern life in his own irreverent style

programme.title: Micky Flanagan's Out Out Tour
programme.desc: A performance by the comedian from his sold-out nationwide tour of 2011. He tells the story of his upbringing in London's East End and a childhood in which alphabetti spaghetti was a luxury, along with his rise to fame, making razor-sharp observations about the British class system along the way

programme.title: Christopher and Holly

programme.title: Nick and Noel
programme.desc: Animated Christmas Special about a girl who wants a mother for Christmas and the cat and dog who make her wish come true

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of Rapunzel, read by Bobby Davro

programme.title: Nanny McPhee
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. A father with seven badly behaved children is in desperate need of some discipline and then a curious stranger comes into their lives

programme.title: The Battle of Billy's Pond (1976)
programme.desc: A boy investigates after his cat becomes ill after eating a dead fish from a local pond. He discovers the fish was killed by industrial waste that a company has been dumping in the water - but the owners will go to any lengths to keep their misdeeds a secret. Family drama, starring Ben Buckton and Andrew Ashby

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec gets on so well with Zoe's parents that she begins to feel left out

programme.title: Gracias Choir Christmas Concert
programme.desc: A special concert of Christmas Carols and Hymns from the Gracias Choir - an award winning choir founded in South Korea

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A journey along the Thames from the Cotswold Hills to the North Sea, concentrating on the lives of the water voles, swans and eels that call it home, and highlighting the escalating battle to control the waterways despite technological advances in flood management. As London continues to sink faster than Venice, the untamed nature of the river is revealed

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Are You Being Served? (1977)
programme.desc: Havoc descends on the coast of Spain as the motley Grace Brothers crew arrive for their annual holiday on the Costa Plonka, which does not exactly live up to the brochure's promise of rest and relaxation. Comedy based on the TV series, starring John Inman, Wendy Richard, Mollie Sugden, Frank Thornton, Trevor Bannister and Arthur English

programme.title: Buttons: A Christmas Tale (2018)
programme.desc: An orphan facing the propspect of life on the street receives a visit from a guardian angel, who comforts her with the story of another child's search for a family and the role another mysterious helper played in her life. Children's fantasy, starring Angela Lansbury and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Oxford Street
programme.desc: A Montage of the famous street around Christmas time in 1970, shot on 16mm film

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank.

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles (2002)
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson set out to protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskerville family, who believes he is being persecuted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the bleak Devon moors. Mystery, starring Richard Roxburgh, Ian Hart, Richard E Grant, John Nettles, Geraldine James, Matt Day and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton

programme.title: Damage (1992)
programme.desc: An English politician at the height of his career risks everything he holds dear when he gives in to his impulses and recklessly embarks on a passionate relationship with his son's girlfriend. When news of the affair reaches Whitehall, the fallout looks set to end his political ambitions. Drama, starring Jeremy Irons, Juliette Binoche, Rupert Graves, and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Emmanuelle in Soho (1981)
programme.desc: A woman seeking fame and fortune in Soho plays a pivotal role in the struggling marriage of a photographer and his wife, and conspires with them on a plot to blackmail a pornography producer. Erotic drama, starring Angie Quick and Julie Lee

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Pretentious Alan, violent Kix and sex-mad Eleanor are part of a 1960s group of poets, artists and angry young men who convene for a weekend of amusement at a large villa overlooking the sea. Comedy, starring Peter Richardson, Adrian Edmondson, Dawn French, Jennifer Saunders and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Cup Fever (1965)
programme.desc: A crooked councillor tries to make life difficult for a children's football team, so they will lose a match against his son's side. However, help is at hand from Manchester United manager Matt Busby. Children's drama, starring David Lodge and Busby as himself, with cameos from a host of football stars

programme.title: Kong: King of Atlantis (2005)
programme.desc: Kong and his 'human' brother Jason travel to Atlantis where Kong has been offered the chance to become King

programme.title: The Prince and the Pauper
programme.desc: Children's adaptation of Mark Twain's famous novel in which the heir to the throne of England switches places with a young peasant boy. John Bowe and Peter Jeffrey star

programme.title: Hide and Seek (1972)
programme.desc: A boy runs away from a young offenders' institute to find his father, whom he discovers is planning a bank robbery. Crime drama, starring Peter Newby and Gary Kemp

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec agrees to give Zoe driving lessons, but it soon puts pressure on their relationship

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Meet the people who have followed their own call of the wild to establish relationships with city animals, and the creatures that have adapted to thrive in this urban jungle

programme.title: Death or Freedom: The Jericho Jailbreak
programme.desc: The story of Operation Jericho, a 1944 mission that saw a squadron of RAF Mosquito bombers try to free French Resistance fighters from a prison in Amiens

programme.title: Moby Dick (1956)
programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

programme.title: For the Love of Ada (1972)
programme.desc: A pensioner goes to great lengths to celebrate her wedding anniversary. Comedy, based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Irene Handl and Wilfred Pickles

programme.title: Spring and Port Wine (1970)
programme.desc: A tyrannically strict Lancashire father brings about the downfall of his family by forcing his daughter to eat a meal against her will. Drama, starring James Mason, Diana Coupland, Susan George and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: The Best of Men
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the birth of the Paralympics in 1948. Pioneering neurologist Dr Ludwig Guttmann arrives at Stoke Mandeville Hospital in Buckinghamshire and begins to transform the lives of his patients - Second World War servicemen with spinal cord injuries. He suggests using sport as a key part of their rehabilitation, and gets them involved in activities including wheelchair polo and basketball. Starring Eddie Marsan, Rob Brydon, George Mackay and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Oxford Street
programme.desc: A Montage of the famous street around Christmas time in 1970, shot on 16mm film

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Series of one-off comedy dramas

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael Caine buys a birthday present for Mrs Huggett. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Krays - Mad Axeman (2019)
programme.desc: Based on the true story of Frank Mitchell who was sprung from Dartmoor Prison by the Krays 10 days before Christmas 1966. Whilst a national manhunt ensued, he was holed up in a flat in East London

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: This That and the Other (1970)
programme.desc: Anthology of three comic tales. A fading movie star tries to seduce a producer, a suicidal loner is snapped out of his misery by a hippy, and a taxi driver suffers a blow to the head that leaves him experiencing erotic hallucinations. Comedy, starring Victor Spinetti, Dennis Waterman, Vanda Hudson and John Bird

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist is joined by Toots Hibbert, frontman of reggae band Toots and the Maytals, who discusses his formative years in Jamaica and some of his favourite records

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: The Battle of Billy's Pond (1976)
programme.desc: A boy investigates after his cat becomes ill after eating a dead fish from a local pond. He discovers the fish was killed by industrial waste that a company has been dumping in the water - but the owners will go to any lengths to keep their misdeeds a secret. Family drama, starring Ben Buckton and Andrew Ashby

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: Thirty delegations from around the world arrive to stay at the hotel for the duration of the Olympics, with the red carpet being rolled out five times a day for heads of state. World-famous Danish eatery Noma sets up a pop-up restaurant in the ballroom and Claridge's chefs are faced with providing Nordic-foraged food to thousands of guests who have paid £195 each. A couple fly in from Atlanta for the wedding of their dreams and a butler prepares a suite for one of the hotel's most valued guests, bringing furniture, hat boxes and 20 suits out of storage for just one night

programme.title: The Man From Nowhere (1975)
programme.desc: A Victorian orphan is sent to live with her uncle, and is troubled by a ghostly presence that haunts his house. Supernatural mystery, starring Sarah Hollis Andrews and Ronald Adam

programme.title: Buttons: A Christmas Tale (2018)
programme.desc: An orphan facing the propspect of life on the street receives a visit from a guardian angel, who comforts her with the story of another child's search for a family and the role another mysterious helper played in her life. Children's fantasy, starring Angela Lansbury and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. Drama, based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, starring Alan Bates, Hayley Mills, Bernard Lee and Norman Bird

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby

programme.title: Red Dog: The Early Years (2016)
programme.desc: A young boy shipped off to his grandad's remote cattle station finds friendship and adventure with a one-of-a-kind dog. Family drama, starring Levi Miller and Jason Isaacs

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era

programme.title: A Song for Jenny
programme.desc: Drama, based on Julie Nicholson's book of the same name, which explores her response to her daughter's death at Edgware Road tube station in the July 7 bombings. The rollercoaster of emotions experienced by Julie and her family after the attack is explored; from hope, denial and acceptance, to grief and the desire to honour Jenny. The programme coincides with the forthcoming 10th anniversary of the 7/7 bombings in London and is a tribute to those who lost their lives in the attack

programme.title: King Willy
programme.desc: A portrait of the famous old Hampstead pub William IV. Observe the action and hear from a number of regulars about their experiences and enjoyment of this establishment

programme.title: The Hitman's Apprentice (2012)
programme.desc: A bumbling teenager is offered a job by his mother's crime boss boyfriend. He is given the task of driving one of the gangster's business associates around town, only to find himself an assistant to a hitman on his last assignment, a mission that leads to a deadly encounter with a mysterious woman. Thriller, starring Tim Roth, Jack O'Connell and Talulah Riley

programme.title: Brimstone and Treacle (1982)
programme.desc: Asinister stranger worms his way into a middle-aged couple's home by offering to take care of their comatose daughter - but it is only once he's part of the family that his true nature shines through. Drama based on Dennis Potter's TV play, starring Sting, Denholm Elliott, Joan Plowright and Suzanna Hamilton

programme.title: Queen of the Blues (1979)
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a failing Soho nightclub and hope to turn its fortunes around - but they end up being threatened by gangsters demanding protection money. Fortunately, salvation is at hand in an enthusiastic stripper, who comes up with a plan to save the day. Drama, starring Mary Millington and Rosemary England

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: World Darts Championship
programme.desc: The afternoon session on day nine of this year's tournament. This stage last year saw Fallon Sherrock shock the darts world with a 3-1 win over Austrian Mensur Suljovic in the second round. That win was Sherrock's second victory in the tournament, after her history-making win over Ted Evetts. Other winners at this stage last year were Australia's Simon Whitlock, and Englishmen Adrian Lewis, Daryl Gurney and Glen Durrant

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Ian Poulter shares his memories of the 2012 Ryder Cup at Medinah

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: The first action from the 39th staging of the biennial contest between teams from the USA and Europe, featuring the foursomes matches at the Medinah Country Club in Illinois, with four points up for grabs. The Americans won the opening session by a point two years ago at the Celtic Manor Resort in Newport, with only the pairing of Lee Westwood and Martin Kaymer claiming a win for Europe

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: Highlights of the second session on day one from the Medinah Country Club in Illinois, featuring the opening fourballs matches. Stewart Cink and Matt Kuchar's victory over Graeme McDowell and Rory McIlroy two years ago in Wales ensured USA held a two-point lead over Europe at the end of the rain-affected second session, but it was the Europeans who went on to win the prestigious title

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: Highlights of the foursomes as the second day's play got under way at the Medinah Country Club in Illinois, with the USA and Europe battling for supremacy. Four matches were scheduled to take place in a session that could ultimately prove crucial, with little to choose between the squads selected by captains Davis Love III and Jose Maria Olazabal

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: Highlights of the foursomes as the second day's play got under way at the Medinah Country Club in Illinois, with the USA and Europe battling for supremacy. Four matches were scheduled to take place in a session that could ultimately prove crucial, with little to choose between the squads selected by captains Davis Love III and Jose Maria Olazabal

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: Action from the 12 singles matches on day three at the Medinah Country Club in Illinois, where the USA and Europe concluded their battle for supremacy. America had dominated the opening two days of foursomes and fourballs matches as they aimed to regain the title, leaving Europe needing an outstanding performance in the singles if they were to have any hope of a fightback

programme.title: Perfection In Paris
programme.desc: A look back at the 2018 Ryder Cup, when Europe and the USA met at Le Golf National in Paris

programme.title: Ryder Cup Golf
programme.desc: Watch along with some of the European players as they look back at coverage of the final, history-making day as Europe and the USA duelled for the Ryder Cup in 2012

programme.desc: A look back at the 39th staging of the event, which took place between the USA and Europe at the Medinah Country Club in Illinois

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Jose Maria Olazabal shares his memories of the 2012 Ryder Cup at Medinah, where he captained the European team to victory in a stunning comeback

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Davis Love III shares his memories of The 2016 Ryder Cup at Hazeltine National Golf Club, at which he captained the American team to their first victory since 2008

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Martin Kaymer shares his memories of the 2012 Ryder Cup at Medinah

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Justin Rose shares his memories of the remarkable 2012 Ryder Cup at Medinah, in which the European team completed one of the sport's great comebacks to take victory

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Luke Donald shares his memories of the remarkable 2012 Ryder Cup in Medinah, where the European team made a staggering comeback to win on the final day. The Americans went into the final day with a 10-6 lead, needing just 4.5 points from the 12 singles matches to win, but the Europeans won eight and tied one of the 12 singles matches to claim a famous 14.5-13.5 victory

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories

programme.title: Ryder Cup Memories
programme.desc: Brandt Snedeker shares his memories of playing at the 2016 Ryder Cup at Hazeltine in Minnesota. Snedeker was part of a team that won the trophy for the first time since 2008

programme.title: Live NFL
programme.desc: New Orleans Saints v Minnesota Vikings (Kick-off 9.30pm). Coverage of the NFC match from Mercedes-Benz Superdome. The home side have put together another winning season so far, despite the absence of talismanic quarterback Drew Brees in recent weeks after he suffered five broken ribs and a collapsed lung, and will be looking for victory here to strengthen their play-off hopes. The Vikings have been inconsistent for much of this campaign, but will hope to progress to the post-season courtesy of a wild card berth
